name: Madhya Pradesh
stopoverNote: Gwalior is a natural northern gateway from Delhi or Agra by rail, Khajuraho has a domestic airport with Delhi links, and Bhopal is the practical exit hub for Mumbai, Delhi and Hyderabad flights.

diet.vegetarian: Madhya Pradesh is very vegetarian-friendly: poha-jalebi, dal bafla, bhutte ka kees, sabudana khichdi, Indori namkeen and Malwi thalis are easy to find across the route.
diet.vegan: Vegan travel is possible with care because ghee, curd and milk are common. Ask for food bilkul bina doodh, ghee, makhan, dahi ke; namkeen, garadu, rice, dal and sabzi are often adaptable.
phrases: Bilkul bina doodh, ghee, makhan, dahi ke = strictly without milk, ghee, butter or curd
Sirf tel mein banao = cook only in oil
Shakahari khana chahiye = I want vegetarian food

cities.name: Gwalior
cities.notes: The Gibraltar of India — an 8th-century hilltop fort with palaces, Jain rock sculptures, temples and the opulent Jai Vilas Palace below.

cities.name: Orchha
cities.notes: A time-frozen Bundela capital on the Betwa River — palaces, riverside cenotaphs and a temple where Ram is worshipped as king.

cities.name: Khajuraho
cities.notes: India's best-known Chandela temple cluster — UNESCO-listed medieval stone carving, celestial sculpture and late-winter dance festival energy.

cities.name: Bhopal
cities.notes: The City of Lakes — MP's leafy capital pairs Nawabi mosques and bazaars with superb tribal and contemporary art museums.

cities.name: Sanchi
cities.notes: Ashoka's 3rd-century Buddhist stupa and carved gateways — one of India's oldest surviving stone monuments and a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

itinerary.sim: Any Indian prepaid SIM such as Jio or Airtel works well across the main route; buy at the airport or an operator store with photo ID. Coverage is solid in Gwalior, Khajuraho and Bhopal, but weaker inside forest reserves.

Gwalior.name: Gwalior
Gwalior.note: One full day covers the fort, palace and old-city tombs; add a morning if Jai Vilas Palace closure or heat slows the plan.

days.theme: Royal Gwalior — hilltop fort to Scindia grandeur
days.note: The fort and palace are 7–8 km apart by auto or cab. Start at the fort early, then move to the palace and old-city tombs; Jai Vilas Palace is closed Mondays.

activities.name: Gwalior Fort Complex — Man Singh Palace, Sas Bahu Temple, Teli Ka Mandir and Jain rock sculptures
activities.tip: Enter via Urwahi Gate by vehicle; a guide for ₹200–₹400 helps decode Man Singh Palace and the underground rooms.

activities.name: Jai Vilas Palace & Scindia Museum
activities.tip: See the Durbar Hall chandeliers and miniature rail dining table; some rooms restrict photography.

activities.name: Tansen Tomb & Gaus Muhammad's Tomb
activities.tip: Visit near sunset; Tansen Samaroh is usually held in Nov–Dec.

activities.name: Light & Sound Show at Gwalior Fort
activities.tip: Hindi and English shows usually run after dark; reconfirm timing at the fort gate.

activities.name: Gujari Mahal Archaeological Museum

Orchha.name: Orchha
Orchha.note: A compact, walkable heritage stop — afternoon arrival plus an overnight covers the fort, temples, chhatris and aarti.

days.theme: Bundela palaces, Betwa chhatris & Ram Raja rituals
days.note: The core sights sit within a 1–2 km walking loop. Keep the palace complex for daylight, then time Ram Raja Temple for aarti.

activities.name: Orchha Fort Complex — Raja Mahal, Jahangir Mahal and Rai Praveen Mahal
activities.tip: Camera fee around ₹25; hire a guide for the 17th-century wall murals.

activities.name: Ram Raja Temple Aarti
activities.tip: Ram is worshipped here as king; dress modestly and expect police-guard ceremony.

activities.name: Chhatris — riverside cenotaphs on the Betwa
activities.tip: Best light is 5–6:30 PM.

activities.name: Chaturbhuj Temple terrace climb
Betwa River boat ride or Orchha Nature Reserve walk
activities.tip: Avoid river activities when the Betwa runs high in monsoon.

meals: Simple vegetarian thali near Ram Raja Temple

Khajuraho.name: Khajuraho
Khajuraho.note: Two days lets you see the UNESCO Western Group properly, then add the eastern/southern temples and a nature excursion.

days.theme: Western Group temples & evening projection show
days.note: The Western Group is the core UNESCO sight. Go early for cooler weather and fewer groups; ASI tickets are best pre-booked or bought before peak hours.

activities.name: Western Group of Temples — Kandariya Mahadeva, Lakshmana, Chitragupta and Vishvanatha
activities.tip: Enter around 6:30–9 AM for golden light; use an ASI-licensed guide for iconography.

activities.name: Khajuraho Sound & Light Show
activities.tip: English show timing shifts by season; arrive 20 minutes early.

activities.name: Khajuraho Archaeological Museum
activities.tip: Useful context for sculptures recovered from the site.

activities.name: Village lanes and craft shops around the temple approach

meals: North Indian restaurant on Jain Temple Road

days.theme: Eastern Jain temples, southern temples & Ken River gorge
days.note: Eastern and Southern Groups are free and quieter. Raneh Falls is 25 km away and works as a half-day nature excursion, best Oct–Jan.

activities.name: Eastern Group — Parsvanatha, Adinatha and Brahma temples
activities.tip: Parsvanatha has some of the finest Jain carving in Khajuraho.

activities.name: Southern Group — Chaturbhuja and Duladeo temples
Raneh Falls & Ken River Gorge
activities.tip: Seasonal waterfalls peak after monsoon; road access can be muddy in heavy rain.

activities.name: Pandav Falls add-on near Panna

meals: Fresh lime soda and snacks after Raneh Falls

Bhopal.name: Bhopal
Bhopal.note: Two days balances lakes, museums and either a UNESCO excursion or a packed Sanchi–Bhimbetka car day.

days.theme: City of Lakes — Bhojtal, tribal art & Van Vihar
days.note: Upper Lake, Van Vihar and the museum cluster are close; Old Bhopal is a separate drive. Van Vihar closes Fridays and museums may close Mondays.

activities.name: Upper Lake (Bhojtal / Bada Talab) boating and lakeside walk
activities.cost: Entry free; cruise boat ₹100, paddle boat ₹80, motor boat ₹240
activities.tip: Sunrise along VIP Road is the prettiest hour; Boat Club usually runs 6 AM–7 PM.

activities.name: Madhya Pradesh Tribal Museum / museum cluster
activities.tip: Excellent Gond, Bhil and Baiga art; check Monday closures.

activities.name: Van Vihar National Park
activities.tip: Closed Fridays; morning and late afternoon give better animal activity.

activities.name: Bharat Bhavan contemporary art space
Chowk Bazaar, Taj-ul-Masajid and Moti Masjid

days.theme: Bhimbetka rock shelters & Old Bhopal culture
days.note: Bhimbetka is 45 km south of Bhopal. Keep it as a half-day unless you have a private car and want to combine Sanchi on a very long UNESCO day.

activities.name: Bhimbetka Rock Shelters (UNESCO WHS)
activities.tip: Open 7 AM–6 PM; wear sturdy shoes for rocky terrain.

activities.name: Drive through Ratapani forest belt viewpoints
activities.tip: Do not plan a tiger-style safari here; this is a heritage-focused excursion.

activities.name: Old Bhopal food walk — chowk lanes and sweets
activities.tip: Vegetarian snacks are easy; ask for no ghee or dahi for vegan versions.

activities.name: Taj-ul-Masajid dawn or sunset photo stop

meals: Dal bafla with ghee on the side

Sanchi.name: Sanchi
Sanchi.note: A classic Bhopal day trip — combine the stupa complex with Udaigiri Caves if using a taxi.

days.theme: Sanchi Stupa, Ashokan gateways & Udaigiri detour
days.note: Sanchi is 50 km from Bhopal. Train is fastest, but a taxi gives flexibility for Udaigiri Caves 7 km away.

activities.name: Sanchi Stupa Complex (UNESCO WHS)
activities.tip: Open daily around 8 AM–7 PM; the museum is usually closed Tuesday. Hire a guide for the Torana narratives.

activities.name: Udaigiri Caves — Gupta-era Varaha and Vishnu panels
activities.cost: ₹25 Indian, ₹300 foreign (ASI standard; verify at site)

activities.name: Sanchi Archaeological Museum
activities.tip: See original gateway fragments and Ashokan material if the museum is open.

activities.name: Return to Bhopal for Upper Lake sunset

hotels.name: Stay in Bhopal and visit Sanchi by day trip

meals: Simple vegetarian lunch near Sanchi station

connections.method: Train to Jhansi plus taxi/auto to Orchha, or direct road taxi
Road taxi is most practical; train via Mahoba is slower
Train such as Mahamana Express or road taxi

extras: Primary 7-day route: Gwalior → Orchha → Khajuraho → Bhopal → Sanchi, using Sanchi as a Bhopal-side day trip but keeping it as its own rule-backed stop.

Khajuraho Western Group, Sanchi Stupa, Gwalior Fort and Bhimbetka are ASI-ticketed; pre-book where possible on the ASI ticket portal and carry ID. Khajuraho Western Group is ₹40 Indian / ₹600 foreign as verified for 2025.

If adding Bandhavgarh or Kanha Tiger Reserve, pre-book safaris 120 days ahead on forest.mponline.gov.in. Core zones close for monsoon from July 1 to about October 14, and safari days should not be combined with heavy sightseeing. Typical jeep cost is ₹4,700–₹5,200 for Indians and ₹5,700–₹6,200 for foreign nationals, subject to the forest portal's live rate.
April–June can reach 42–46°C across the plateau. July–September brings monsoon disruption and reserve closures; Pachmarhi is the main cooler exception but is outside this 7-day route.

Madhya Pradesh is highly vegetarian-friendly. Poha-jalebi, dal bafla, bhutte ka kees, sabudana khichdi and namkeen make easy snacks between heritage stops; vegan travelers should explicitly request no ghee, milk, butter or curd.
